brief_summary: Side-by-side cross-section of ABS and PBT keycaps. Left (ABS): thicker ~1.5mm walls, smooth glossy top surface, with a shine-progression strip below showing amber filling four quadrants from 'new' through '24 mo'. Right (PBT): slightly thinner ~1.3mm walls, faintly textured top indicated by short stipple marks, shine-progression strip stays nearly empty across the same timespan.
data_sources: keycap-materials-compared.mdx § 'ABS' — 'GMK sets typically run 1.5mm thick — produce a low, focused sound the community calls thocky ... Most ABS sets show legible shine on the home row in 6 to 18 months of daily typing'

keycap-materials-compared.mdx § 'PBT' — 'PBT walls flex less. PBT surfaces resist the polishing process ... A well-made PBT set looks effectively the same after three years that it did out of the box ... PBT has a faintly textured surface, almost matte'
warnings: PBT 1.3mm wall is a representative range, not article-specified — used as a relative comparison against the article's stated 1.5mm for GMK ABS
